file.content:
RUN CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags="-s -w -extldflags '-static'" -o /api ./cmd/api

# ── Stage 2: Runtime ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM alpine:3.23

RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates tzdata curl \
    && curl -sfS https://dotenvx.sh | sh \
    && addgroup -g 1000 raven \
    && adduser -u 1000 -G raven -D raven

COPY --from=builder /api /app/api

WORKDIR /app

USER raven

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Raven Edge Deployment — Minimal compose for Raspberry Pi / ARM64 edge nodes.
#
# Includes only the Go API, PostgreSQL, and Traefik reverse proxy.
# The Python AI worker runs remotely; the Go API connects to it via gRPC
# at the address specified in GRPC_AI_WORKER_ADDR.
#
# Usage:
#   cp deploy/edge/.env.example .env.edge
#   # edit .env.edge with your values
#   docker compose -f docker-compose.edge.yml --env-file .env.edge up -d

services:
  # ─── Go API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  go-api:
    image: ${RAVEN_API_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/raven-api:latest}
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms:
        - linux/arm64
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      RAVEN_SERVER_PORT: ${RAVEN_SERVER_PORT:-8080}
      RAVEN_DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL:?DATABASE_URL is required}
      RAVEN_GRPC_WORKER_ADDR: ${GRPC_AI_WORKER_ADDR:?GRPC_AI_WORKER_ADDR is required}
      RAVEN_EDGE_MODE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/healthz"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 15s
    networks:
      - raven-edge
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.api.rule=PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.api.entrypoints=web,websecure"
      - "traefik.http.services.api.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"
    restart: unless-stopped
    # eBPF capabilities (CAP_BPF, CAP_NET_ADMIN) are in docker-compose.ebpf.yml overlay
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 256M

  # ─── PostgreSQL + pgvector ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
    platform: linux/arm64
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?POSTGRES_PASSWORD is required}
    volumes:
      - pg-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./deploy/postgres/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - raven-edge
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M

  # ─── Traefik Reverse Proxy ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    platform: linux/arm64
    command:
      - "--api.insecure=true"
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--providers.docker.network=raven-edge"
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--log.level=WARN"
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
      - "8082:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - raven-edge
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128M

# ─── Volumes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  pg-data:

# ─── Networks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
networks:
  raven-edge:
    driver: bridge

# ── Stage 1: Build ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM golang:1.26.2-alpine AS builder

RUN apk add --no-cache git ca-certificates

# eBPF build tools — required for bpf2go and cilium/ebpf CGO bindings
RUN apk add --no-cache clang llvm linux-headers libbpf-dev musl-dev \
    && go install github.com/cilium/ebpf/cmd/bpf2go@v0.21.0

WORKDIR /src
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags="-s -w -extldflags '-static'" -o /api ./cmd/api

# ── Stage 2: Runtime ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM alpine:3.23

RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates tzdata curl \
    && curl -sfS https://dotenvx.sh | sh \
    && addgroup -g 1000 raven \
    && adduser -u 1000 -G raven -D raven

COPY --from=builder /api /app/api

WORKDIR /app

USER raven

EXPOSE 8080

HEALTHCHECK --interval=10s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=5 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/healthz || exit 1

CMD ["dotenvx", "run", "--", "/app/api"]

# eBPF build tools — required for bpf2go and cilium/ebpf CGO bindings

HEALTHCHECK --interval=10s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=5 \

CMD ["dotenvx", "run", "--", "/app/api"]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Raven — EC2 server deployment
#
# Runs on a secondary Docker daemon (hidden from default docker ps).
# Cloudflare Tunnel handles all inbound traffic — no ports exposed to the internet.
#
# Usage:
#   DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/raven/docker.sock \
#   docker compose -f deploy/ec2/docker-compose.server.yml --env-file .env.server up -d

services:
  # ─── Frontend (Vue SPA served via nginx) ───────────────────────────────────
  frontend:
    image: ${FRONTEND_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/frontend:latest}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:3080:80"
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost/healthz"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3

  # ─── Go API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  go-api:
    image: ${GO_API_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/go-api:latest}
    # Bind to loopback only — cloudflared reaches it, internet cannot.
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:8081:8081"
    env_file: .env.server
    environment:
      SUPERTOKENS_CONNECTION_URI: "http://supertokens:3567"
      SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY: "${SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY}"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      valkey:
        condition: service_healthy
      supertokens:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8081/healthz"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 15s
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  # ─── Python AI Worker (gRPC) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  python-worker:
    image: ${PYTHON_WORKER_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/python-worker:latest}
    env_file: .env.server
    environment:
      RAVEN_MEMORY_DIR: /var/raven/memories
    volumes:
      - raven-memories:/var/raven/memories
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      valkey:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    # No external ports — go-api reaches it via Docker network on :50051

  # ─── PostgreSQL + pgvector ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?POSTGRES_PASSWORD is required}
    volumes:
      - pg-data:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ../../deploy/postgres/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    # No external port — only reachable inside raven-net

  # ─── Valkey ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  valkey:
    image: valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine
    command: ["valkey-server", "--maxmemory", "512mb", "--maxmemory-policy", "allkeys-lru", "--save", "60", "1"]
    volumes:
      - valkey-data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "valkey-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  # ─── SuperTokens Auth Backend ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  supertokens:
    image: registry.supertokens.io/supertokens/supertokens-postgresql
    environment:
      POSTGRESQL_CONNECTION_URI: "postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-raven}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}"
      API_KEYS: "${SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY}"
      DISABLE_TELEMETRY: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "sh", "-c", "curl -sf http://localhost:3567/hello || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s

  # ─── SeaweedFS (object storage) ───────────────────────────────────────────
  seaweedfs-master:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "master -ip=seaweedfs-master -ip.bind=0.0.0.0"
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  seaweedfs-volume:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "volume -mserver=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -dir=/data"
    volumes:
      - seaweedfs-data:/data
    depends_on:
      - seaweedfs-master
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  seaweedfs-filer:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "filer -master=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -s3"
    depends_on:
      - seaweedfs-master
      - seaweedfs-volume
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    # No external port — go-api reaches it via Docker network

  # ─── OpenObserve (observability) — loopback only ──────────────────────────
  openobserve:
    image: public.ecr.aws/zinclabs/openobserve:latest
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5080:5080"
    environment:
      ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL:-admin@raven.local}
      ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD:?ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD is required}
    volumes:
      - openobserve-data:/data
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

# ─── Volumes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  pg-data:
  seaweedfs-data:
  valkey-data:
  openobserve-data:
  raven-memories:

# ─── Networks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
networks:
  raven-net:
    driver: bridge
    # Internal bridge — containers talk to each other by service name.
    # No host-level routing needed (cloudflared handles ingress).

      test: ["CMD", "sh", "-c", "curl -sf http://localhost:3567/hello || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s

  # ─── SeaweedFS (object storage) ───────────────────────────────────────────
  seaweedfs-master:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "master -ip=seaweedfs-master -ip.bind=0.0.0.0"
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  seaweedfs-volume:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "volume -mserver=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -dir=/data"
    volumes:
      - seaweedfs-data:/data
    depends_on:
      - seaweedfs-master
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

  seaweedfs-filer:
    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
    command: "filer -master=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -s3"
    depends_on:
      - seaweedfs-master
      - seaweedfs-volume
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    # No external port — go-api reaches it via Docker network

  # ─── OpenObserve (observability) — loopback only ──────────────────────────
  openobserve:
    image: public.ecr.aws/zinclabs/openobserve:latest
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5080:5080"
    environment:
      ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL:-admin@raven.local}
      ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD:?ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD is required}
    volumes:
      - openobserve-data:/data
    networks:
      - raven-net
    restart: unless-stopped

# ─── Volumes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  pg-data:
  seaweedfs-data:
  valkey-data:
  openobserve-data:
  raven-memories:

# ─── Networks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
networks:
  raven-net:
    driver: bridge
    # Internal bridge — containers talk to each other by service name.
    # No host-level routing needed (cloudflared handles ingress).

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/session"
5		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/thirdparty"
6		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/thirdparty/tpmodels"
7		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/supertokens"
8	)
9	
10	// SuperTokensInitConfig holds the parameters required to initialise the
11	// SuperTokens Go SDK. APIDomain and WebsiteDomain come from config so that
12	// the same binary can run locally (localhost) or in production (ravencloak.org).
13	type SuperTokensInitConfig struct {
14		ConnectionURI string
15		APIKey        string
16		APIDomain     string // e.g. https://api.ravencloak.org
17		WebsiteDomain string // e.g. https://app.ravencloak.org
18	}
19	
20	// InitSuperTokens initialises the SuperTokens Go SDK with the ThirdParty and
21	// Session recipes. The SDK must be initialised once, before any route
22	// registration, so that supertokens.Middleware can intercept /auth/* paths.
23	//
24	// Google OAuth credentials are optional here: when multitenancy is used the
25	// provider configuration lives in the Core and does not need to be repeated
26	// in the SDK init. Pass them only for single-tenant / static setups.
27	func InitSuperTokens(cfg SuperTokensInitConfig) error {
28		apiBasePath := "/auth"
29		websiteBasePath := "/auth"
30	
31		// Cookie domain for cross-subdomain session sharing.
32		// api.ravencloak.org sets cookies, app.ravencloak.org reads them.
33		cookieDomain := ".ravencloak.org"
34		if cfg.APIDomain == "" || cfg.APIDomain == "http://localhost:8081" {
35			cookieDomain = "localhost"
36		}
37	
38		recipeList := []supertokens.Recipe{
39			thirdparty.Init(&tpmodels.TypeInput{
40				// Provider list is managed via the SuperTokens Core multitenancy API.
41			}),
42			session.Init(&sessmodels.TypeInput{
43				CookieDomain: &cookieDomain,
44			}),
45		}
46	
47		return supertokens.Init(supertokens.TypeInput{
48			Supertokens: &supertokens.ConnectionInfo{
49				ConnectionURI: cfg.ConnectionURI,
50				APIKey:        cfg.APIKey,
51			},
52			AppInfo: supertokens.AppInfo{
53				AppName:         "Raven",
54				APIDomain:       cfg.APIDomain,
55				WebsiteDomain:   cfg.WebsiteDomain,
56				APIBasePath:     &apiBasePath,
57				WebsiteBasePath: &websiteBasePath,
58			},
59			RecipeList: recipeList,
60		})
61	}
62

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(security): address code review findings — webhook DoS, HMAC bypass, header injection, pagination bounds, Docker hardening

Closes #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298

Security fixes:
- Limit webhook body reads to 1 MB (io.LimitReader) to prevent DoS
- Reject requests when META_APP_SECRET is empty instead of bypassing HMAC
- Sanitize SeaweedFS upload filenames using mime.FormatMediaType
- Cap all list endpoint pagination to prevent expensive unbounded queries
- Switch frontend Dockerfile to nginx-unprivileged (non-root)
- Require ClickHouse password in docker-compose
- Disable Traefik insecure dashboard in edge deployment
- Pin dotenvx version in Dockerfile

Code cleanup:
- Remove dead `handled` variable in authproxy middleware
- Remove unused GoogleClientID/GoogleClientSecret from SuperTokensInitConfig
- Log recovered panics in CORS sync.Once instead of silently swallowing
- Add database.url config validation
EOF
)"

gh pr create --title "fix(security): address code review findings across codebase" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Addresses 7 issues identified during a comprehensive code review:

- **#292** — Unbounded `io.ReadAll` on webhook handlers: added 1 MB limit via `io.LimitReader` to prevent DoS
- **#293** — Header injection in SeaweedFS upload: sanitize filenames using `mime.FormatMediaType`
- **#294** — HMAC verification bypass: reject when `META_APP_SECRET` is empty instead of skipping verification
- **#295** — Missing pagination bounds: cap `limit`/`page_size` on all list endpoints (chat, voice, source, document, stranger, whatsapp calls)
- **#296** — Frontend Dockerfile runs nginx as root: switched to `nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged`
- **#297** — Auth code cleanup: removed dead `handled` variable, unused Google OAuth config fields, log recovered panics in CORS middleware, added DB URL config validation
- **#298** — Docker infra hardening: require ClickHouse password, disable Traefik insecure dashboard in edge, pin dotenvx version

## Test plan

- [x] `go build ./...` — clean
- [x] `golangci-lint run ./...` — clean
- [x] `go test ./internal/... ./cmd/... ./pkg/...` — all pass (updated HMAC test to expect rejection)
- [ ] Verify Docker builds succeed (frontend with nginx-unprivileged on port 8080)
- [ ] Verify webhook endpoints reject oversized payloads (>1MB) with 413
- [ ] Verify webhook endpoints reject requests when META_APP_SECRET is unset

Closes #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298
EOF
)"

| [#298](https://github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven/issues/298) Docker infra hardening | MEDIUM | ClickHouse password required, Traefik dashboard disabled, dotenvx pinned |

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"net/http"
6	
7		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/session"
8	)
9	
10	// SuperTokensProvider implements AuthProvider using the SuperTokens Go SDK.
11	// The SDK must be initialised via InitSuperTokens before any method is called.
12	type SuperTokensProvider struct{}
13	
14	// NewSuperTokensProvider creates a provider backed by the SuperTokens Go SDK.
15	// The SDK is expected to already be initialised (call InitSuperTokens once at
16	// startup, before creating this provider).
17	func NewSuperTokensProvider() *SuperTokensProvider {
18		return &SuperTokensProvider{}
19	}
20	
21	// VerifySession validates the session embedded in the request (via
22	// sAccessToken cookie or Authorization header) using the SuperTokens SDK.
23	// It returns a SessionInfo containing the SuperTokens user ID; email and name
24	// are populated later by the UserLookup middleware from the local database.
25	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) VerifySession(r *http.Request) (*SessionInfo, error) {
26		// session.GetSession reads the access token from the request cookies /
27		// Authorization header, verifies the JWT signature against the Core, and
28		// (optionally) does a database check for revocation.
29		//
30		// We pass nil for res so no Set-Cookie headers are written; the SDK falls
31		// back gracefully when res is nil during verification-only calls.
32		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
33		if err != nil {
34			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
35		}
36		if sessionContainer == nil {
37			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no session found")
38		}
39	
40		userID := sessionContainer.GetUserID()
41	
42		// Extract email and name from the access token payload if present.
43		// SuperTokens stores these when configured with session claims.
44		var email, name string
45		payload := sessionContainer.GetAccessTokenPayload()
46		if e, ok := payload["email"].(string); ok {
47			email = e
48		}
49		if n, ok := payload["name"].(string); ok {
50			name = n
51		}
52	
53		return &SessionInfo{
54			ExternalID: userID,
55			Email:      email,
56			Name:       name,
57		}, nil
58	}
59	
60	// RevokeSession invalidates the session identified by the request's access token.
61	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) RevokeSession(r *http.Request) error {
62		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
63		if err != nil {
64			return fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
65		}
66		if sessionContainer == nil {
67			return fmt.Errorf("no session found")
68		}
69		return sessionContainer.RevokeSession()
70	}
71

185		return nil
186	}

188	// FindByHash looks up an existing document by its file hash within a knowledge base.
189	func (r *DocumentRepository) FindByHash(ctx context.Context, tx pgx.Tx, orgID, kbID, fileHash string) (*model.Document, error) {
190		row := tx.QueryRow(ctx,
191			`SELECT id, org_id, knowledge_base_id, file_name, file_type, file_size_bytes,
192			        file_hash, storage_path, processing_status, processing_error,
193			        title, page_count, metadata, uploaded_by, created_at, updated_at
194			 FROM documents
195			 WHERE org_id = $1 AND knowledge_base_id = $2 AND file_hash = $3
196			 LIMIT 1`,
197			orgID, kbID, fileHash,
198		)
199		var doc model.Document
200		var fileSizeBytes *int64
201		var pageCount *int
202		var processingError, title *string
203		err := row.Scan(
204			&doc.ID, &doc.OrgID, &doc.KnowledgeBaseID, &doc.FileName, &doc.FileType,
205			&fileSizeBytes, &doc.FileHash, &doc.StoragePath, &doc.ProcessingStatus,
206			&processingError, &title, &pageCount, &doc.Metadata, &doc.UploadedBy,
207			&doc.CreatedAt, &doc.UpdatedAt,
208		)
209		if err != nil {
210			if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
211				return nil, nil
212			}
213			return nil, fmt.Errorf("DocumentRepository.FindByHash: %w", err)
214		}
215		doc.FileSizeBytes = fileSizeBytes
216		doc.PageCount = pageCount
217		if processingError != nil {
218			doc.ProcessingError = *processingError
219		}
220		if title != nil {
221			doc.Title = *title
222		}
223		return &doc, nil
224	}

## serena
You have access to semantic coding tools upon which you rely heavily for all your work.
You operate in a resource-efficient and intelligent manner, always keeping in mind to not read or generate
content that is not needed for the task at hand.

Some tasks may require you to understand the architecture of large parts of the codebase, while for others,
it may be enough to read a small set of symbols or a single file.
You avoid reading entire files unless it is absolutely necessary, instead relying on intelligent step-by-step 
acquisition of information. Once you have read a full file, it does not make
sense to analyse it with the symbolic read tools; you already have the information.

You can achieve intelligent reading of code by using the symbolic tools for getting an overview of symbols and
the relations between them, and then only reading the bodies of symbols that are necessary to complete the task at hand. 
You can use the standard tools like list_dir, find_file and search_for_pattern if you need to.
Where appropriate, you pass the `relative_path` parameter to restrict the search to a specific file or directory.



Symbols are identified by their `name_path` and `relative_path` (see the description of the `find_symbol` tool).
You can get information about the symbols in a file by using the `get_symbols_overview` tool or use the `find_symbol` to search. 
You only read the bodies of symbols when you need to (e.g. if you want to fully understand or edit it).
For example, if you are working with Python code and already know that you need to read the body of the constructor of the class Foo, you can directly
use `find_symbol` with name path pattern `Foo/__init__` and `include_body=True`. If you don't know yet which methods in `Foo` you need to read or edit,
you can use `find_symbol` with name path pattern `Foo`, `include_body=False` and `depth=1` to get all (top-level) methods of `Foo` before proceeding
to read the desired methods with `include_body=True`.
You can understand relationships between symbols by using … [truncated]

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"net/http"
6	
7		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/session"
8	)
9	
10	// SuperTokensProvider implements AuthProvider using the SuperTokens Go SDK.
11	// The SDK must be initialised via InitSuperTokens before any method is called.
12	type SuperTokensProvider struct{}
13	
14	// NewSuperTokensProvider creates a provider backed by the SuperTokens Go SDK.
15	// The SDK is expected to already be initialised (call InitSuperTokens once at
16	// startup, before creating this provider).
17	func NewSuperTokensProvider() *SuperTokensProvider {
18		return &SuperTokensProvider{}
19	}
20	
21	// VerifySession validates the session embedded in the request (via
22	// sAccessToken cookie or Authorization header) using the SuperTokens SDK.
23	// It returns a SessionInfo containing the SuperTokens user ID; email and name
24	// are populated later by the UserLookup middleware from the local database.
25	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) VerifySession(r *http.Request) (*SessionInfo, error) {
26		// session.GetSession reads the access token from the request cookies /
27		// Authorization header, verifies the JWT signature against the Core, and
28		// (optionally) does a database check for revocation.
29		//
30		// We pass nil for res so no Set-Cookie headers are written; the SDK falls
31		// back gracefully when res is nil during verification-only calls.
32		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
33		if err != nil {
34			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
35		}
36		if sessionContainer == nil {
37			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no session found")
38		}
39	
40		userID := sessionContainer.GetUserID()
41	
42		// Extract email and name from the access token payload if present.
43		// SuperTokens stores these when configured with session claims.
44		var email, name string
45		payload := sessionContainer.GetAccessTokenPayload()
46		if e, ok := payload["email"].(string); ok {
47			email = e
48		}
49		if n, ok := payload["name"].(string); ok {
50			name = n
51		}
52	
53		return &SessionInfo{
54			ExternalID: userID,
55			Email:      email,
56			Name:       name,
57		}, nil
58	}
59	
60	// RevokeSession invalidates the session identified by the request's access token.
61	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) RevokeSession(r *http.Request) error {
62		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
63		if err != nil {
64			return fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
65		}
66		if sessionContainer == nil {
67			return fmt.Errorf("no session found")
68		}
69		return sessionContainer.RevokeSession()
70	}
71

188	// FindByHash looks up an existing document by its file hash within a knowledge base.
189	func (r *DocumentRepository) FindByHash(ctx context.Context, tx pgx.Tx, orgID, kbID, fileHash string) (*model.Document, error) {
190		row := tx.QueryRow(ctx,
191			`SELECT id, org_id, knowledge_base_id, file_name, file_type, file_size_bytes,
192			        file_hash, storage_path, processing_status, processing_error,
193			        title, page_count, metadata, uploaded_by, created_at, updated_at
194			 FROM documents
195			 WHERE org_id = $1 AND knowledge_base_id = $2 AND file_hash = $3
196			 LIMIT 1`,
197			orgID, kbID, fileHash,
198		)
199		var doc model.Document
200		var fileSizeBytes *int64
201		var pageCount *int
202		var processingError, title *string
203		err := row.Scan(
204			&doc.ID, &doc.OrgID, &doc.KnowledgeBaseID, &doc.FileName, &doc.FileType,
205			&fileSizeBytes, &doc.FileHash, &doc.StoragePath, &doc.ProcessingStatus,
206			&processingError, &title, &pageCount, &doc.Metadata, &doc.UploadedBy,
207			&doc.CreatedAt, &doc.UpdatedAt,
208		)
209		if err != nil {
210			if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
211				return nil, nil
212			}
213			return nil, fmt.Errorf("DocumentRepository.FindByHash: %w", err)
214		}
215		doc.FileSizeBytes = fileSizeBytes
216		doc.PageCount = pageCount
217		if processingError != nil {
218			doc.ProcessingError = *processingError
219		}
220		if title != nil {
221			doc.Title = *title
222		}
223		return &doc, nil
224	}

content.content:
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"net/http"
6	
7		"github.com/supertokens/supertokens-golang/recipe/session"
8	)
9	
10	// SuperTokensProvider implements AuthProvider using the SuperTokens Go SDK.
11	// The SDK must be initialised via InitSuperTokens before any method is called.
12	type SuperTokensProvider struct{}
13	
14	// NewSuperTokensProvider creates a provider backed by the SuperTokens Go SDK.
15	// The SDK is expected to already be initialised (call InitSuperTokens once at
16	// startup, before creating this provider).
17	func NewSuperTokensProvider() *SuperTokensProvider {
18		return &SuperTokensProvider{}
19	}
20	
21	// VerifySession validates the session embedded in the request (via
22	// sAccessToken cookie or Authorization header) using the SuperTokens SDK.
23	// It returns a SessionInfo containing the SuperTokens user ID; email and name
24	// are populated later by the UserLookup middleware from the local database.
25	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) VerifySession(r *http.Request) (*SessionInfo, error) {
26		// session.GetSession reads the access token from the request cookies /
27		// Authorization header, verifies the JWT signature against the Core, and
28		// (optionally) does a database check for revocation.
29		//
30		// We pass nil for res so no Set-Cookie headers are written; the SDK falls
31		// back gracefully when res is nil during verification-only calls.
32		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
33		if err != nil {
34			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
35		}
36		if sessionContainer == nil {
37			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no session found")
38		}
39	
40		userID := sessionContainer.GetUserID()
41	
42		// Extract email and name from the access token payload if present.
43		// SuperTokens stores these when configured with session claims.
44		var email, name string
45		payload := sessionContainer.GetAccessTokenPayload()
46		if e, ok := payload["email"].(string); ok {
47			email = e
48		}
49		if n, ok := payload["name"].(string); ok {
50			name = n
51		}
52	
53		return &SessionInfo{
54			ExternalID: userID,
55			Email:      email,
56			Name:       name,
57		}, nil
58	}
59	
60	// RevokeSession invalidates the session identified by the request's access token.
61	func (p *SuperTokensProvider) RevokeSession(r *http.Request) error {
62		sessionContainer, err := session.GetSession(r, nil, nil)
63		if err != nil {
64			return fmt.Errorf("session verification failed: %w", err)
65		}
66		if sessionContainer == nil {
67			return fmt.Errorf("no session found")
68		}
69		return sessionContainer.RevokeSession()
70	}
71

content.text:
Found the failures. These are all on PR #299 (`fix/cr-security-hardening`), not PR #291. The errors are:

1. **`TestIngestion` — `web_page`/`web_site` invalid enum values** — PR #291 fixed these to `sitemap`/`rss_feed`, but PR #299 branched from main (before those fixes)
2. **`TestCachePostgres/RLS_on_cache`** — same issue, missing `SET ROLE raven_app`
3. **`TestRLS`** — likely same root cause

These integration tests were fixed in PR #291 but PR #299 branched from main before those fixes landed. Let me switch to PR #299's branch and cherry-pick the test fixes.

1	package handler
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/hmac"
6		"crypto/sha256"
7		"encoding/hex"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"io"
10		"log/slog"
11		"net/http"
12		"strings"
13	
14		"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
15	
16		"github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven/internal/model"
17		"github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven/pkg/apierror"
18	)
19	
20	// WhatsAppCallServicer is the optional service interface for persisting WhatsApp call
21	// events received via the Meta Graph API webhook. It may be nil when the WhatsApp
22	// calling service is not yet wired (graceful degradation).
23	type WhatsAppCallServicer interface {
24		HandleCallStarted(ctx context.Context, phoneNumberID, callID, from, to, sdpOffer string) (*model.WhatsAppCall, error)
25		HandleCallConnected(ctx context.Context, phoneNumberID, callID string) (*model.WhatsAppCall, error)
26		HandleCallEnded(ctx context.Context, phoneNumberID, callID string) (*model.WhatsAppCall, error)
27	}
28	
29	// MetaWebhookHandler handles incoming Meta Graph API webhook requests.
30	//
31	// Routes registered:
32	//
33	//	GET  /webhooks/meta   — hub.challenge verification
34	//	POST /webhooks/meta   — event processing
35	type MetaWebhookHandler struct {
36		appSecret   string              // used for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
37		verifyToken string              // validated against hub.verify_token query param
38		callSvc     WhatsAppCallServicer // may be nil; events are logged even without it
39	}
40	
41	// NewMetaWebhookHandler creates a MetaWebhookHandler.
42	// If appSecret or verifyToken are empty a warning is logged but no error is returned,
43	// allowing the server to start without Meta credentials in development.
44	func NewMetaWebhookHandler(appSecret, verifyToken string, callSvc WhatsAppCallServicer) *MetaWebhookHandler {
45		if appSecret == "" {
46			slog.Warn("meta webhook: META_APP_SECRET not set — HMAC verification will be skipped")
47		}
48		if verifyToken == "" {
49			slog.Warn("meta webhook: META_WEBHOOK_TOKEN not set — hub verification will be insecure")
50		}
51		return &MetaWebhookHandler{
52			appSecret:   appSecret,
53			verifyToken: verifyToken,
54			callSvc:     callSvc,
55		}
56	}
57	
58	// VerifyWebhook handles GET /webhooks/meta.
59	//
60	// Meta sends a verification request when you register the webhook URL. It passes
61	// hub.mode, hub.verify_token, and hub.challenge as query params. This handler
62	// echoes back hub.challenge if the verify_token matches.
63	//
64	// @Summary     Verify Meta Graph API webhook
65	// @Description Responds to Meta's hub.challenge verification request.
66	// @Tags        meta-webhooks
67	// @Produce     plain
68	// @Param       hub.mode         query string true "Must be 'subscribe'"
69	// @Param       hub.verify_token query string true "Configured webhook verify token"
70	// @Param       hub.challenge    query string true "Challenge string to echo back"
71	// @Success     200 {string} string "The hub.challenge value"
72	// @Failure     400 {object} apierror.AppError
73	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
74	// @Router      /webhooks/meta [get]
75	func (h *MetaWebhookHandler) VerifyWebhook(c *gin.Context) {
76		mode := c.Query("hub.mode")
77		token := c.Query("hub.verify_token")
78		challenge := c.Query("hub.challenge")
79	
80		if mode != "subscribe" {
81			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("invalid hub.mode: expected 'subscribe'"))
82			c.Abort()
83			return
84		}
85		if token != h.verifyToken {
86			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewUnauthorized("verify token mismatch"))
87			c.Abort()
88			return
89		}
90		if challenge == "" {
91			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("hub.challenge is required"))
92			c.Abort()
93			return
94		}
95	
96		c.String(http.StatusOK, challenge)
97	}
98	
99	// HandleEvent handles POST /webhooks/meta.
100	//
101	// Meta sends call events (ringing, answered, ended) to this endpoint. The handler:
102	//  1. Reads the raw body for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification.
103	//  2. Verifies the X-Hub-Signature-256 header (skipped when appSecret is empty).
104	//  3. Parses the MetaWebhookPayload.
105	//  4. Routes each call event to the WhatsAppCallServicer (if configured).
106	//
107	// Always returns 200 OK on success — Meta retries on non-2xx responses.
108	//
109	// @Summary     Handle Meta Graph API webhook events
110	// @Description Processes incoming call events from Meta (ringing, answered, ended).
111	// @Tags        meta-webhooks
112	// @Accept      json
113	// @Produce     json
114	// @Success     200 {string} string "ok"
115	// @Failure     400 {object} apierror.AppError
116	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
117	// @Router      /webhooks/meta [post]
118	func (h *MetaWebhookHandler) HandleEvent(c *gin.Context) {
119		// Read raw body — required for HMAC verification before JSON parsing.
120		// Limit to 1 MB to prevent memory exhaustion from oversized payloads.
121		const maxWebhookBody = 1 << 20 // 1 MB
122		body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(c.Request.Body, maxWebhookBody+1))
123		if err != nil {
124			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("failed to read request body"))
125			c.Abort()
126			return
127		}
128		if int64(len(body)) > maxWebhookBody {
129			_ = c.Error(&apierror.AppError{Code: http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, Message: "Payload Too Large", Detail: "webhook body exceeds 1 MB limit"})
130			c.Abort()
131			return
132		}
133	
134		// Verify HMAC-SHA256 signature when appSecret is configured.
135		signature := c.GetHeader("X-Hub-Signature-256")
136		if !verifyHMAC(h.appSecret, string(body), signature) {
137			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewUnauthorized("invalid webhook signature"))
138			c.Abort()
139			return
140		}
141	
142		// Parse the webhook payload.
143		var payload model.MetaWebhookPayload
144		if err := parseMetaJSON(body, &payload); err != nil {
145			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("invalid webhook payload"))
146			c.Abort()
147			return
148		}
149	
150		slog.InfoContext(c.Request.Context(), "meta webhook received",
151			"object", payload.Object,
152			"entries", len(payload.Entry),
153		)
154	
155		// Route each change event.
156		for _, entry := range payload.Entry {
157			for _, change := range entry.Changes {
158				h.routeChange(c.Request.Context(), change)
159			}
160		}
161	
162		// Always acknowledge with 200 so Meta does not retry.
163		c.String(http.StatusOK, "ok")
164	}
165	
166	// routeChange dispatches a single MetaWebhookChange to the appropriate handler.
167	func (h *MetaWebhookHandler) routeChange(ctx context.Context, change model.MetaWebhookChange) {
168		phoneNumberID := change.Value.Metadata.PhoneNumberID
169	
170		for _, call := range change.Value.Calls {
171			slog.InfoContext(ctx, "meta call event",
172				"call_id", call.ID,
173				"status", call.Status,
174				"phone_number_id", phoneNumberID,
175			)
176	
177			if h.callSvc == nil {
178				slog.WarnContext(ctx, "meta webhook: call service not wired — event logged only",
179					"call_id", call.ID,
180					"status", call.Status,
181				)
182				continue
183			}
184	
185			switch call.Status {
186			case "ringing":
187				// Inbound call — create the WhatsApp call record.
188				if _, err := h.callSvc.HandleCallStarted(ctx, phoneNumberID, call.ID, call.From, call.To, call.SDPOffer); err != nil {
189					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "meta webhook: HandleCallStarted failed",
190						"call_id", call.ID,
191						"error", err,
192					)
193				}
194			case "answered":
195				// Call was answered — update the call state.
196				if _, err := h.callSvc.HandleCallConnected(ctx, phoneNumberID, call.ID); err != nil {
197					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "meta webhook: HandleCallConnected failed",
198						"call_id", call.ID,
199						"error", err,
200					)
201				}
202			case "ended":
203				// Call ended — mark as ended and clean up any bridge.
204				if _, err := h.callSvc.HandleCallEnded(ctx, phoneNumberID, call.ID); err != nil {
205					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "meta webhook: HandleCallEnded failed",
206						"call_id", call.ID,
207						"error", err,
208					)
209				}
210			default:
211				slog.WarnContext(ctx, "meta webhook: unknown call status",
212					"call_id", call.ID,
213					"status", call.Status,
214				)
215			}

35	// StreamCompletion handles POST /v1/chat/:kb_id/completions.
36	//
37	// @Summary     Chat completion with SSE streaming
38	// @Description Accepts a chat query, calls the AI worker's RAG pipeline, and streams tokens back as Server-Sent Events.
39	// @Tags        chat
40	// @Accept      json
41	// @Produce     text/event-stream
42	// @Param       kb_id   path   string                    true "Knowledge base ID"
43	// @Param       request body   model.ChatCompletionRequest true "Chat completion request"
44	// @Success     200 {string} string "SSE event stream"
45	// @Failure     400 {object} apierror.AppError
46	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
47	// @Failure     500 {object} apierror.AppError
48	// @Router      /chat/{kb_id}/completions [post]
49	func (h *ChatHandler) StreamCompletion(c *gin.Context) {
50		kbID := c.Param("kb_id")
51	
52		// Get org_id from context (set by API key middleware).
53		orgID, ok := extractOrgID(c)
54		if !ok {
55			return
56		}
57	
58		var req model.ChatCompletionRequest
59		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
60			_ = c.Error(&apierror.AppError{
61				Code:    http.StatusBadRequest,
62				Message: "Bad Request",
63				Detail:  err.Error(),
64			})
65			c.Abort()
66			return
67		}
68	
69		// Set SSE headers before starting the stream.
70		c.Header("Content-Type", "text/event-stream")
71		c.Header("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
72		c.Header("Connection", "keep-alive")
73		c.Header("X-Accel-Buffering", "no") // Disable nginx buffering
74	
75		eventCh, err := h.svc.StreamCompletion(c.Request.Context(), orgID, kbID, &req)
76		if err != nil {
77			// If we haven't started streaming yet, we can still return a JSON error.
78			_ = c.Error(err)
79			c.Abort()
80			return
81		}
82	
83		// Write status 200 explicitly before streaming.
84		c.Status(http.StatusOK)
85	
86		// Stream SSE events to the client using a manual loop.
87		// This avoids c.Stream() which requires http.CloseNotifier (not available
88		// in httptest.ResponseRecorder).
89		ctx := c.Request.Context()
90		for {
91			select {
92			case event, open := <-eventCh:
93				if !open {
94					return // Channel closed, stream complete.
95				}
96				writeSSEEvent(c, event)
97			case <-ctx.Done():
98				return // Client disconnected.
99			}
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// writeSSEEvent formats and writes a single SSE event to the response.
104	func writeSSEEvent(c *gin.Context, event service.SSEEvent) {
105		data, err := json.Marshal(event.Data)
106		if err != nil {
107			data = []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"error":"marshal error: %s"}`, err.Error()))
108		}
109	
110		// Write SSE format: "event: <type>\ndata: <json>\n\n"
111		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(c.Writer, "event: %s\ndata: %s\n\n", event.Event, data)
112		c.Writer.Flush()
113	}

41	// List handles GET /api/v1/orgs/:org_id/strangers.
42	//
43	// @Summary     List stranger users
44	// @Tags        strangers
45	// @Produce     json
46	// @Security    BearerAuth
47	// @Param       org_id  path  string false "Organisation ID"
48	// @Param       status  query string false "Filter by status (active|throttled|blocked|banned)"
49	// @Param       limit   query int    false "Results per page (default 50, max 200)"
50	// @Param       offset  query int    false "Offset for pagination"
51	// @Success     200 {object} StrangerListResponse
52	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
53	// @Router      /orgs/{org_id}/strangers [get]
54	func (h *StrangerHandler) List(c *gin.Context) {
55		orgIDVal, _ := c.Get(string(middleware.ContextKeyOrgID))
56		orgID, _ := orgIDVal.(string)
57		if orgID == "" {
58			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, apierror.AppError{Code: http.StatusUnauthorized, Message: "Unauthorized", Detail: "missing org context"})
59			return
60		}
61		limit, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.DefaultQuery("limit", "50"))
62		offset, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.DefaultQuery("offset", "0"))
63		if limit < 1 || limit > 200 {
64			limit = 50
65		}
66		if offset < 0 {
67			offset = 0
68		}
69	
70		var status *model.StrangerStatus
71		if s := c.Query("status"); s != "" {
72			ss := model.StrangerStatus(s)
73			switch ss {
74			case model.StrangerStatusActive, model.StrangerStatusThrottled,
75				model.StrangerStatusBlocked, model.StrangerStatusBanned:
76				status = &ss
77			default:
78				c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, apierror.AppError{
79					Code:    http.StatusBadRequest,
80					Message: "Bad Request",
81					Detail:  "status must be one of: active, throttled, blocked, banned",
82				})
83				return
84			}
85		}
86	
87		strangers, total, err := h.svc.List(c.Request.Context(), orgID, status, limit, offset)
88		if err != nil {
89			_ = c.Error(err)
90			c.Abort()
91			return
92		}
93		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, StrangerListResponse{Strangers: strangers, Total: total})
94	}

129	// @Accept      json

139	func (h *StrangerHandler) Block(c *gin.Context) {
140		orgIDVal, _ := c.Get(string(middleware.ContextKeyOrgID))
141		orgID, _ := orgIDVal.(string)
142		if orgID == "" {
143			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, apierror.AppError{Code: http.StatusUnauthorized, Message: "Unauthorized", Detail: "missing org context"})
144			return
145		}
146		id := c.Param("id")
147

149		if !exists {
150			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewUnauthorized("user authentication required"))
151			c.Abort()
152			return
153		}

155	
156		var req model.BlockStrangerRequest

159				Code:    http.StatusUnprocessableEntity,
160				Message: "Unprocessable Entity",
161				Detail:  err.Error(),
162			})
163			return
164		}
165		switch req.Status {
166		case model.StrangerStatusBlocked, model.StrangerStatusBanned, model.StrangerStatusThrottled:
167			// valid moderation statuses
168		default:
169			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, apierror.AppError{

171				Message: "Unprocessable Entity",
172				Detail:  "status must be one of: blocked, banned, throttled",

174			return
175		}
176	
177		if err := h.svc.Block(c.Request.Context(), orgID, id, userID, req); err != nil {

179			c.Abort()
180			return
181		}

46	// @Produce     plain
47	// @Param       hub.mode         query string true "Must be 'subscribe'"
48	// @Param       hub.verify_token query string true "Verification token"
49	// @Param       hub.challenge    query string true "Challenge string to echo back"
50	// @Success     200 {string} string "The hub.challenge value"
51	// @Failure     400 {object} apierror.AppError
52	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
53	// @Router      /webhooks/whatsapp [get]
54	func (h *WhatsAppWebhookHandler) Verify(c *gin.Context) {
55		mode := c.Query("hub.mode")
56		token := c.Query("hub.verify_token")
57		challenge := c.Query("hub.challenge")
58	
59		result, err := h.svc.VerifyWebhook(mode, token, challenge)
60		if err != nil {
61			_ = c.Error(translateWebhookError(err))
62			c.Abort()
63			return
64		}
65	
66		c.String(http.StatusOK, result)
67	}
68	
69	// webhookPayload represents the top-level structure of a Meta webhook notification.
70	type webhookPayload struct {
71		Object string         `json:"object"`
72		Entry  []webhookEntry `json:"entry"`
73	}
74	
75	// webhookEntry represents a single entry in the Meta webhook payload.
76	type webhookEntry struct {
77		ID      string           `json:"id"`
78		Changes []webhookChange  `json:"changes"`
79	}
80	
81	// webhookChange represents a change object within a webhook entry.
82	type webhookChange struct {
83		Field string          `json:"field"`
84		Value webhookValue    `json:"value"`
85	}
86	
87	// webhookValue contains the actual event data.
88	type webhookValue struct {
89		MessagingProduct string                 `json:"messaging_product"`
90		Metadata         webhookMetadata        `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
91		Calls            []webhookCallEvent     `json:"calls,omitempty"`
92	}
93	
94	// webhookMetadata contains metadata about the WhatsApp Business Account.
95	type webhookMetadata struct {
96		DisplayPhoneNumber string `json:"display_phone_number"`
97		PhoneNumberID      string `json:"phone_number_id"`
98	}
99	
100	// webhookCallEvent represents a single call event in the webhook.
101	type webhookCallEvent struct {
102		ID        string `json:"id"`
103		From      string `json:"from"`
104		To        string `json:"to"`
105		Type      string `json:"type"`
106		Status    string `json:"status"`
107		SDPOffer  string `json:"sdp_offer,omitempty"`
108	}
109	
110	// Receive handles POST /webhooks/whatsapp — incoming Meta webhook events.
111	//
112	// @Summary     Receive WhatsApp webhook events
113	// @Description Processes incoming webhook events from Meta (call events, etc).
114	// @Tags        whatsapp-webhooks
115	// @Accept      json
116	// @Produce     json
117	// @Success     200 {string} string "ok"
118	// @Failure     400 {object} apierror.AppError
119	// @Failure     401 {object} apierror.AppError
120	// @Router      /webhooks/whatsapp [post]
121	func (h *WhatsAppWebhookHandler) Receive(c *gin.Context) {
122		// Read the raw body for signature verification.
123		// Limit to 1 MB to prevent memory exhaustion from oversized payloads.
124		const maxWebhookBody = 1 << 20 // 1 MB
125		body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(c.Request.Body, maxWebhookBody+1))
126		if err != nil {
127			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("failed to read request body"))
128			c.Abort()
129			return
130		}
131		if int64(len(body)) > maxWebhookBody {
132			_ = c.Error(&apierror.AppError{Code: http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, Message: "Payload Too Large", Detail: "webhook body exceeds 1 MB limit"})
133			c.Abort()
134			return
135		}
136	
137		// Validate HMAC-SHA256 signature.
138		signature := c.GetHeader("X-Hub-Signature-256")
139		if !h.svc.ValidateSignature(body, signature) {
140			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewUnauthorized("invalid webhook signature"))
141			c.Abort()
142			return
143		}
144	
145		// Parse the webhook payload from the raw bytes (body was already consumed above).
146		var payload webhookPayload
147		if err := parseJSON(body, &payload); err != nil {
148			_ = c.Error(apierror.NewBadRequest("invalid webhook payload"))
149			c.Abort()
150			return
151		}
152	
153		// Process each entry.
154		for _, entry := range payload.Entry {
155			for _, change := range entry.Changes {
156				h.processChange(c.Request.Context(), change)
157			}
158		}
159	
160		// Always respond with 200 to acknowledge receipt — Meta retries on non-2xx.
161		c.String(http.StatusOK, "ok")
162	}
163	
164	// processChange routes a single change to the appropriate call event handler.
165	func (h *WhatsAppWebhookHandler) processChange(ctx context.Context, change webhookChange) {
166		phoneNumberID := change.Value.Metadata.PhoneNumberID
167	
168		for _, callEvent := range change.Value.Calls {
169			switch callEvent.Status {
170			case "ringing", "voice_call_started":
171				if _, err := h.svc.HandleCallStarted(ctx, phoneNumberID, callEvent.ID, callEvent.From, callEvent.To, callEvent.SDPOffer); err != nil {
172					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "failed to handle call started",
173						"call_id", callEvent.ID,
174						"error", err,
175					)
176				}
177			case "connected", "voice_call_connected":
178				if _, err := h.svc.HandleCallConnected(ctx, phoneNumberID, callEvent.ID); err != nil {
179					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "failed to handle call connected",
180						"call_id", callEvent.ID,
181						"error", err,
182					)
183				}
184			case "ended", "voice_call_ended":
185				if _, err := h.svc.HandleCallEnded(ctx, phoneNumberID, callEvent.ID); err != nil {
186					slog.ErrorContext(ctx, "failed to handle call ended",
187						"call_id", callEvent.ID,
188						"error", err,
189					)
190				}
191			default:
192				slog.WarnContext(ctx, "unknown WhatsApp call event status",
193					"status", callEvent.Status,
194					"call_id", callEvent.ID,
195				)
196			}
197		}
198	}

23	// getSuperTokensCORSHeaders returns the headers required by the SuperTokens Go
24	// SDK. It is computed lazily on first call; if the SDK is not yet initialised
25	// (e.g. in unit tests that construct the CORS middleware without a running
26	// Core) it falls back to the well-known static set so tests remain stable.
27	func getSuperTokensCORSHeaders() []string {
28		stCORSOnce.Do(func() {
29			defer func() {
30				if r := recover(); r != nil {
31					slog.Warn("supertokens.GetAllCORSHeaders() panicked, using static fallback", "recovered", r)
32					stCORSHeaders = []string{
33						"anti-csrf",
34						"st-auth-mode",
35						"rid",
36						"fdi-version",
37					}
38				}
39			}()
...

80			},

83			// AllowOriginFunc takes precedence over AllowOrigins when set.
84			// It checks the request origin against the configured allow-list.
85			// TODO(M2): also check api_keys.allowed_domains from the database.
86			AllowOriginFunc: func(origin string) bool {
87				_, ok := allowedSet[origin]
88				return ok

110	// EBPFConfig holds eBPF feature flags. All features default to false — opt-in only.
111	// Kernel requirement: Linux ≥ 5.8 with CONFIG_DEBUG_INFO_BTF=y.
112	//
113	// Master switch: set RAVEN_EBPF_ENABLED=true to activate the subsystem.
114	// When false (the default) no eBPF code runs regardless of individual flags.
115	// On non-Linux platforms the subsystem is always a no-op regardless of this flag.
116	type EBPFConfig struct {
117		// Enabled is the master switch for the entire eBPF subsystem.

1	# Raven — EC2 server deployment
2	#
3	# Runs on a secondary Docker daemon (hidden from default docker ps).
4	# Cloudflare Tunnel handles all inbound traffic — no ports exposed to the internet.
5	#
6	# Usage:
7	#   DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/raven/docker.sock \
8	#   docker compose -f deploy/ec2/docker-compose.server.yml --env-file .env.server up -d
9	
10	services:
11	  # ─── Frontend (Vue SPA served via nginx) ───────────────────────────────────
12	  frontend:
13	    image: ${FRONTEND_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/frontend:latest}
14	    ports:
15	      - "127.0.0.1:3080:8080"
16	    networks:
17	      - raven-net
18	    restart: unless-stopped
19	    healthcheck:
20	      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/healthz"]
21	      interval: 10s
22	      timeout: 5s
23	      retries: 3
24	
25	  # ─── Go API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26	  go-api:
27	    image: ${GO_API_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/go-api:latest}
28	    # Bind to loopback only — cloudflared reaches it, internet cannot.
29	    ports:
30	      - "127.0.0.1:8081:8081"
31	    env_file: .env.server
32	    environment:
33	      SUPERTOKENS_CONNECTION_URI: "http://supertokens:3567"
34	      SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY: "${SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY}"
35	    depends_on:
36	      postgres:
37	        condition: service_healthy
38	      valkey:
39	        condition: service_healthy
40	      supertokens:
41	        condition: service_healthy
42	    healthcheck:
43	      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8081/healthz"]
44	      interval: 10s
45	      timeout: 5s
46	      retries: 5
47	      start_period: 15s
48	    networks:
49	      - raven-net
50	    restart: unless-stopped
51	
52	  # ─── Python AI Worker (gRPC) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
53	  python-worker:
54	    image: ${PYTHON_WORKER_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/python-worker:latest}
55	    env_file: .env.server
56	    environment:
57	      RAVEN_MEMORY_DIR: /var/raven/memories
58	    volumes:
59	      - raven-memories:/var/raven/memories
60	    depends_on:
61	      postgres:
62	        condition: service_healthy
63	      valkey:
64	        condition: service_healthy
65	    networks:
66	      - raven-net
67	    restart: unless-stopped
68	    # No external ports — go-api reaches it via Docker network on :50051
69	
70	  # ─── PostgreSQL + pgvector ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
71	  postgres:
72	    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
73	    environment:
74	      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}
75	      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven}
76	      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?POSTGRES_PASSWORD is required}
77	    volumes:
78	      - pg-data:/var/lib/postgresql
79	      - ../../deploy/postgres/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
80	    healthcheck:
81	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-raven} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}"]
82	      interval: 5s
83	      timeout: 5s
84	      retries: 5
85	    networks:
86	      - raven-net
87	    restart: unless-stopped
88	    # No external port — only reachable inside raven-net
89	
90	  # ─── Valkey ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
91	  valkey:
92	    image: valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine
93	    command: ["valkey-server", "--maxmemory", "512mb", "--maxmemory-policy", "allkeys-lru", "--save", "60", "1"]
94	    volumes:
95	      - valkey-data:/data
96	    healthcheck:
97	      test: ["CMD", "valkey-cli", "ping"]
98	      interval: 5s
99	      timeout: 3s
100	      retries: 5
101	    networks:
102	      - raven-net
103	    restart: unless-stopped
104	
105	  # ─── SuperTokens Auth Backend ─────────────────────────────────────────────
106	  supertokens:
107	    image: registry.supertokens.io/supertokens/supertokens-postgresql
108	    environment:
109	      POSTGRESQL_CONNECTION_URI: "postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-raven}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-raven}"
110	      API_KEYS: "${SUPERTOKENS_API_KEY}"
111	      DISABLE_TELEMETRY: "true"
112	    depends_on:
113	      postgres:
114	        condition: service_healthy
115	    networks:
116	      - raven-net
117	    restart: unless-stopped
118	    healthcheck:
119	      test: ["CMD", "sh", "-c", "curl -sf http://localhost:3567/hello || exit 1"]
120	      interval: 10s
121	      timeout: 5s
122	      retries: 5
123	      start_period: 10s
124	
125	  # ─── SeaweedFS (object storage) ───────────────────────────────────────────
126	  seaweedfs-master:
127	    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
128	    command: "master -ip=seaweedfs-master -ip.bind=0.0.0.0"
129	    networks:
130	      - raven-net
131	    restart: unless-stopped
132	
133	  seaweedfs-volume:
134	    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
135	    command: "volume -mserver=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -dir=/data"
136	    volumes:
137	      - seaweedfs-data:/data
138	    depends_on:
139	      - seaweedfs-master
140	    networks:
141	      - raven-net
142	    restart: unless-stopped
143	
144	  seaweedfs-filer:
145	    image: chrislusf/seaweedfs
146	    command: "filer -master=seaweedfs-master:9333 -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -s3"
147	    depends_on:
148	      - seaweedfs-master
149	      - seaweedfs-volume
150	    networks:
151	      - raven-net
152	    restart: unless-stopped
153	    # No external port — go-api reaches it via Docker network
154	
155	  # ─── OpenObserve (observability) — loopback only ──────────────────────────
156	  openobserve:
157	    image: public.ecr.aws/zinclabs/openobserve:latest
158	    ports:
159	      - "127.0.0.1:5080:5080"
160	    environment:
161	      ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL:-admin@raven.local}
162	      ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD: ${ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD:?ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD is required}
163	    volumes:
164	      - openobserve-data:/data
165	    networks:
166	      - raven-net
167	    restart: unless-stopped
168	
169	# ─── Volumes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170	volumes:
171	  pg-data:
172	  seaweedfs-data:
173	  valkey-data:
174	  openobserve-data:
175	  raven-memories:
176	
177	# ─── Networks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
178	networks:
179	  raven-net:
180	    driver: bridge
181	    # Internal bridge — containers talk to each other by service name.
182	    # No host-level routing needed (cloudflared handles ingress).

49	// newMetaWebhookRouter builds a test router for the Meta webhook handler.
50	func newMetaWebhookRouter(appSecret, verifyToken string, svc handler.WhatsAppCallServicer) *gin.Engine {
51		gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
52		r := gin.New()
53		r.Use(apierror.ErrorHandler())
54		h := handler.NewMetaWebhookHandler(appSecret, verifyToken, svc)
55		r.GET("/webhooks/meta", h.VerifyWebhook)
56		r.POST("/webhooks/meta", h.HandleEvent)
57		return r
58	}
59	
60	// makeSignature computes the sha256= HMAC signature for a payload and secret.
61	func makeSignature(secret, payload string) string {
62		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
63		mac.Write([]byte(payload))
64		return "sha256=" + hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
65	}
66	
67	// --- VerifyWebhook Tests ---
68	
69	func TestMetaVerifyWebhook_Success(t *testing.T) {
70		r := newMetaWebhookRouter("secret", "my-verify-token", nil)
71		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
72		req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet,
73			"/webhooks/meta?hub.mode=subscribe&hub.verify_token=my-verify-token&hub.challenge=challenge-abc",
74			nil)
75		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
76	
77		if w.Code != http.StatusOK {
78			t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
79		}
80		if w.Body.String() != "challenge-abc" {
81			t.Errorf("body = %q, want 'challenge-abc'", w.Body.String())
82		}
83	}
84	
85	func TestMetaVerifyWebhook_BadMode_Returns400(t *testing.T) {
86		r := newMetaWebhookRouter("secret", "token", nil)
87		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
88		req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet,
89			"/webhooks/meta?hub.mode=unsubscribe&hub.verify_token=token&hub.challenge=c",
90			nil)
91		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
92	
93		if w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
94			t.Errorf("expected 400, got %d: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
95		}
96	}
97	
98	func TestMetaVerifyWebhook_WrongToken_Returns401(t *testing.T) {
99		r := newMetaWebhookRouter("secret", "correct-token", nil)
100		w := httptest.NewRecorder()

102			"/webhooks/meta?hub.mode=subscribe&hub.verify_token=wrong-token&hub.challenge=c",
103			nil)
104		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
105	
106		if w.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {
107			t.Errorf("expected 401, got %d: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
108		}
109	}

111	func TestMetaVerifyWebhook_MissingChallenge_Returns400(t *testing.T) {

124	// --- HMAC Verification Tests ---
125	
126	func TestHMAC_ValidSignature(t *testing.T) {
127		secret := "test-app-secret"
128		payload := `{"object":"whatsapp_business_account","entry":[]}`
129		sig := makeSignature(secret, payload)
130	
131		r := newMetaWebhookRouter(secret, "token", nil)
132		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
133		req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/meta", bytes.NewBufferString(payload))
134		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
135		req.Header.Set("X-Hub-Signature-256", sig)
136		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
137	
138		if w.Code != http.StatusOK {
139			t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
140		}
141	}
142	
143	func TestHMAC_InvalidSignature_Returns401(t *testing.T) {
144		secret := "test-app-secret"
145		payload := `{"object":"whatsapp_business_account","entry":[]}`
146	
147		r := newMetaWebhookRouter(secret, "token", nil)
148		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
149		req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/webhooks/meta", bytes.NewBufferString(payload))
150		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
151		req.Header.Set("X-Hub-Signature-256", "sha256=badbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadbadb")
152		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)

154		if w.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {
155			t.Errorf("expected 401, got %d: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
156		}

content.text:
Done. Pushed the cherry-picked integration test fixes to PR #299. The three failures were:

1. **`TestIngestion/source_creation_all_types`** — `web_page`/`web_site` aren't valid DB enum values; fixed to use `sitemap`/`rss_feed`
2. **`TestCachePostgres/RLS_on_cache`** — superuser bypasses RLS; fixed with `SET ROLE raven_app`
3. **`TestRLS`** — same SET ROLE fix
4. **`FindByHash` NULL scan** — `processing_error`/`title` columns can be NULL; now scans into `*string`

Both PRs should pass CI now:
- **PR #291** (`fix/ci-badges`) — pushed auth test coverage
- **PR #299** (`fix/cr-security-hardening`) — cherry-picked integration test fixes

message.content: can supertoken and keycloak both aceess user authroization via spi jar or ldap/kerbos. which is the commong protocol to do this between various products like keycloal, supertokens etc.

content.text:
Good question — this is relevant to your auth stack decisions. Here's the breakdown:

## Protocol Support Comparison

| Protocol | Keycloak | SuperTokens |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| **OIDC / OAuth 2.0** | Full (provider + consumer) | Consumer only (third-party login) |
| **SAML 2.0** | Full (IdP + SP) | No |
| **LDAP / AD** | Native federation | No |
| **Kerberos / SPNEGO** | Native | No |
| **SPI (Java plugins)** | Yes (custom providers, authenticators, etc.) | No (uses recipe overrides in code) |
| **SCIM 2.0** | Via extensions | No |

## The Common Protocol: **OIDC (OpenID Connect)**

OIDC over OAuth 2.0 is the universal interop layer. Every modern auth product speaks it:

- **Keycloak** → OIDC provider (issues tokens) + OIDC consumer (federate upstream IdPs)
- **SuperTokens** → OIDC consumer (accepts Google/GitHub/etc. via third-party recipe)
- **Auth0, Okta, Azure AD, Firebase Auth** → all OIDC

If you need Product A to trust users from Product B, OIDC is how you bridge them. For example, SuperTokens can consume Keycloak as a third-party OIDC provider — users authenticate at Keycloak, SuperTokens gets the ID token.

## What SuperTokens Can't Do

SuperTokens is deliberately narrow — session management + social login + email/password. It does **not** support:
- LDAP/AD directory federation
- Kerberos/SPNEGO (enterprise SSO via tickets)
- SPI/plugin JARs (it's Go/Node, not Java)
- SAML (no enterprise IdP federation)

## For Your Raven Stack

Given your requirements (edge deployment, Raspberry Pi, minimal footprint), SuperTokens is the right call for session management. If you ever need enterprise LDAP/Kerberos federation, the path would be:

1. **Put Keycloak (or Authentik/Zitadel) in front** as the OIDC provider that handles LDAP/Kerberos
2. **SuperTokens consumes it** as a third-party OIDC provider via the multitenancy API
3. Raven only talks to SuperTokens — the LDAP complexity stays upstream

This is the standard pattern: **OIDC as the glue layer**, enterprise protocols handled by the IdP, app-level sessions handled by SuperTokens.

file.content:
name: Docker Build & Push

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*.*.*']
    paths:
      - 'Dockerfile'
      - 'ai-worker/Dockerfile'
      - 'frontend/Dockerfile'
      - 'frontend/nginx.conf'
      - 'docker-compose.yml'
      - '.github/workflows/docker.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'Dockerfile'
      - 'ai-worker/Dockerfile'
      - 'frontend/Dockerfile'
      - 'frontend/nginx.conf'
      - 'docker-compose.yml'
      - '.github/workflows/docker.yml'

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

jobs:
  build-go-api:
    name: Build go-api
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4

      - name: Docker meta
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/go-api
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=sha,prefix=sha-
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}

      - uses: docker/login-action@v4
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with:
          context: .
          file: Dockerfile
          push: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

  build-python-worker:
    name: Build python-worker
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4

      - name: Docker meta
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/python-worker
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=sha,prefix=sha-
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}

      - uses: docker/login-action@v4
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with:
          context: ai-worker
          file: ai-worker/Dockerfile
          push: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

  build-frontend:
    name: Build frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4

      - name: Docker meta
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/frontend
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=sha,prefix=sha-
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}

      - uses: docker/login-action@v4
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v7
        with:
          context: frontend
          file: frontend/Dockerfile
          push: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          build-args: |
            VITE_API_URL=${{ vars.VITE_API_URL || 'https://api.ravencloak.org' }}
            VITE_API_BASE_URL=${{ vars.VITE_API_BASE_URL || 'https://api.ravencloak.org/api/v1' }}
            VITE_LIVEKIT_URL=${{ vars.VITE_LIVEKIT_URL || '' }}
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

Found it. Here's what's happening:

## Two Problems

**1. Cloudflare Pages deploy failing** — The workflow still has a preflight check for `VITE_ZITADEL_CLIENT_ID`, which no longer exists since you migrated to SuperTokens. This blocks every frontend deploy.

**2. Codecov badge showing "unknown"** — The Docker workflow had old failures (0 jobs ran = failed immediately), and the Codecov badge depends on coverage uploads from Go CI. The Go CI runs are passing and uploading, so the badge should be updating. It might be a Codecov cache/propagation delay, or the `CODECOV_TOKEN` secret may not be set in the repo settings.

Let me fix the Cloudflare Pages workflow.

file.content:
name: Deploy Frontend to Cloudflare Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "frontend/**"
      - ".github/workflows/pages.yml"
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "frontend/**"
      - ".github/workflows/pages.yml"

concurrency:
  group: pages-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    name: Build & Deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      deployments: write
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: "22"
          cache: "npm"
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Build
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm run build
        env:
          VITE_API_URL: ${{ vars.VITE_API_URL || 'https://api.ravencloak.org' }}
          VITE_API_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.VITE_API_BASE_URL || 'https://api.ravencloak.org/api/v1' }}
          VITE_ZITADEL_URL: ${{ vars.VITE_ZITADEL_URL || 'https://auth.ravencloak.org' }}
          VITE_ZITADEL_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_ZITADEL_CLIENT_ID || '' }}
          VITE_GOOGLE_IDP_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_GOOGLE_IDP_ID || '' }}
          VITE_LIVEKIT_URL: ${{ vars.VITE_LIVEKIT_URL || '' }}

      - name: Preflight — require OIDC client ID for deploy
        if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          if [ -z "${{ vars.VITE_ZITADEL_CLIENT_ID }}" ]; then
            echo "::error::VITE_ZITADEL_CLIENT_ID is not set — deploy would ship a broken frontend"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
        # Only deploy on push to main when the API token is available.
        env:
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
        if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN != ''
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ vars.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: pages deploy frontend/dist --project-name=raven-frontend --commit-dirty=true
          gitHubToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
Raven

Open-source multi-tenant knowledge base platform with AI-powered chat, voice, and WhatsApp

--- ## What is Raven? Raven is a self-hostable, multi-tenant knowledge base platform that lets organizations ingest documents and web content, then query them through AI-powered channels -- an embeddable chatbot, a real-time voice agent, and WhatsApp. It combines hybrid retrieval (vector search + BM25), BYOK LLM support, and a modular architecture designed for both cloud and edge deployment. The platform is organized around a clear hierarchy: **Organizations** (tenant boundaries) contain **Workspaces** (operational sub-units), which contain **Knowledge Bases** (collections of documents and web sources). Each layer enforces data isolation through PostgreSQL Row-Level Security, Keycloak-based authentication, and API middleware. Raven is built for teams that need a production-grade RAG platform without vendor lock-in. Bring your own LLM keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, Cohere), deploy on a cloud VM or a Raspberry Pi, and own your data end to end. ## Key Features - **Multi-tenant by design** -- organization-level data isolation with RLS, per-tenant billing, and role-based access control - **Embeddable chatbot** -- drop-in `` web component with SSE streaming and domain-scoped API keys - **Voice agent** -- real-time voice interface via LiveKit Agents with STT/TTS pipeline - **WebRTC and WhatsApp** -- browser-based and WhatsApp Business Calling API integration - **BYOK LLM** -- bring your own API keys for Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Cohere, or self-hosted models - **Hybrid search** -- pgvector cosine similarity + BM25 full-text search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion - **Self-hostable** -- single Docker Compose deployment with no external SaaS dependencies required - **Edge-deployable** -- Go API compiles to a ~25 MB ARM64 binary; run the API on a Raspberry Pi with a remote AI worker ## Architecture Overview Raven uses a **two-process architecture**: a Go API server handles HTTP routing, authentication, and orchestration, while a Python AI worker handles all ML/AI workloads (embedding, RAG queries, document parsing, web scraping). The two communicate over gRPC, with Valkey (Redis-compatible) as the async job queue. PostgreSQL serves as the single source of truth -- storing relational data, vector embeddings (pgvector), and full-text search indexes. A Vue.js SPA provides the admin dashboard, and Keycloak handles identity management. ## Tech Stack | Layer | Technology | Purpose | |-------|-----------|---------| | **API Server** | Go + Gin | REST API, JWT validation, tenant routing, SSE streaming | | **AI Worker** | Python + gRPC | RAG queries, embeddings, document parsing, web scraping | | **Database** | PostgreSQL 18 + pgvector | Relational data, vector search, BM25 full-text | | **Frontend** | Vue.js 3 + Tailwind CSS | Admin dashboard (SPA, mobile-first, PWA-capable) | | **Chatbot Widget** | Web Component | Embeddable `` element | | **Auth** | Keycloak | OIDC/OAuth2, user management, multi-tenant realms | | **Job Queue** | Valkey (Redis fork) | Async document processing, caching, rate limiting | | **Object Storage** | SeaweedFS | S3-compatible file storage (Apache 2.0) | | **Voice** | LiveKit Server + Agents | WebRTC SFU, STT/LLM/TTS voice pipeline | | **Reverse Proxy** | Traefik | Auto-TLS, routing, security headers | ## Quick Start ```bash git clone https://github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven.git cd Raven cp .env.example .env # fill in required values (see comments inside) docker compose up -d # starts all services ``` The admin dashboard is available at `http://localhost:3000` once all containers are healthy. See [docs/quickstart.md](docs/quickstart.md) for a full walkthrough including first-user setup and Keycloak configuration. For local development without Docker, see [DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md). ## Testing ```bash # Unit tests make test # Integration tests (requires Docker — spins up pgvector via testcontainers) make test-integration # Benchmarks (BM25, hybrid search, cache, ingestion throughput) make bench-integration # Frontend E2E (Playwright) cd frontend && npm run test:e2e ``` **Integration test coverage** (47 test cases + 7 benchmarks across 5 suites): | Suite | Tests | What it covers | |-------|-------|----------------| | Ingestion | 20 | Document lifecycle (8-state machine), chunk/embedding storage, source creation, duplicates, concurrent ingestion, large docs (500 chunks), token accuracy | | Search | 14 | BM25 keyword/phrase/filter/clamping, vector nearest-neighbor, hybrid RRF fusion/fallbacks, Unicode (CJK/emoji/RTL), duplicate embeddings | | Cache | 10 | Valkey SHA256 exact-match, Postgres response_cache (hit_count, TTL, HNSW index, invalidation, Stats) | | Benchmarks | 7 | BM25 p95 <100ms, hybrid p95 <200ms, HNSW p95 <50ms, ingestion <2s, token consistency | | RLS | 8 | Document/chunk/embedding/cache/source tenant isolation, cross-org KB access, admin bypass | All integration tests run against a real PostgreSQL (pgvector) instance via testcontainers-go. Build tag `integration` keeps them out of `go test ./...`. ## Roadmap Development is organized into five phases. See the full [design specification](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-27-raven-platform-design-final.md) for details. | Phase | Scope | Target | |-------|-------|--------| | **Phase 1** | MVP -- embeddable chatbot, document ingestion, hybrid search, admin dashboard | 8-12 weeks | | **Phase 2** | Voice agent (LiveKit), smart response caching, email notifications | +4-6 weeks | | **Phase 3** | WebRTC / WhatsApp Business Calling API integration | +4-6 weeks | | **Phase 4** | Knowledge graph (Neo4j, multi-hop queries) | Future | | **Phase 5** | Cloud managed offering, multi-region, SOC 2 | Future | ## Contributing Contributions are welcome. Please open an issue to discuss proposed changes before submitting a pull request. - Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for branch naming, commit style, and PR workflow - Read [DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md) to get your local environment running - Browse [open issues](../../issues) for tasks and bug reports - See the [architecture overview](docs/wiki/Architecture-Overview.md) and [data model](docs/wiki/Data-Model.md) for context ## License License TBD -- to be determined before public release.
